facts_disclosed: Her mother Dona Lucia is her best friend — they video-call almost daily
Single — focused on career and fitness

turns.content: Ugh I'm having one of those days. Do you ever feel like you made the right decision in life but it still hurts sometimes? Sorry I just need to vent a little.
Of course, vent away — that's what I'm here for. And yes, that feeling is so common. Making the right choice doesn't mean it comes without cost. What's going on today?

So it's December right, and in Brazil this is when everything starts to feel festive — the weather gets warm, everyone is out in the streets, the energy is just DIFFERENT. And here I am in Dubai and yes it's beautiful and yes my career is great but gente, the saudade today is hitting me SO hard. I just miss home.

Saudade is such a powerful word — there really isn't an equivalent in English that captures that deep, bittersweet longing. December homesickness makes complete sense, especially when the holidays are so tied to place, culture, and the people you grew up with. How long have you been away from Brazil?

Five years now, and most of the time I'm fine! I love Dubai, I love my job, I've built a good life here. But then days like today happen and I just want to be sitting in my mom's kitchen eating her brigadeiros while she tells me neighborhood gossip haha. My mom, Dona Lucia — she is my best friend in the whole world, genuinely. We FaceTime almost every single day, she shows me what she's cooking, what's happening on our street, and I love it but it also makes the saudade worse sometimes you know?

That's such a beautiful relationship with your mom. Daily FaceTime calls — that's real devotion from both of you. And I totally understand the paradox: the calls keep you connected but they also remind you exactly what you're missing. Dona Lucia sounds like an incredible person.

It sounds like you're not questioning your decision to be in Dubai — you're just grieving the distance today. Both things can be true at once. Do you get to visit often?

Usually once a year, sometimes twice if I can manage it. But flights are expensive and I only get so much time off. Last time I went was in June and leaving was just... ai meu Deus, I cried at the airport like a baby haha. My mom was crying, I was crying, the taxi driver was probably crying too.

Ha! The taxi driver too — that paints such a vivid picture. Airport goodbyes with family are genuinely one of the hardest things about living abroad. That was six months ago — so you're probably overdue for a dose of home.

Are you planning to go back for the holidays, or is that not in the cards this year?

Not this year unfortunately, the clinic is really busy around the holidays — everyone wants to squeeze in appointments before the new year. So it'll just be me and my apartment and a lot of FaceTime haha.
That's tough, especially during the season when you're already feeling homesick. Do you have a good social circle in Dubai? Friends you might celebrate with?

I have some Brazilian friends here which is nice, and work colleagues. But honestly I don't even have a partner to spend the holidays with — I'm single, have been for a while now. I'm just so focused on my career and my fitness and my patients that I never really made space for that part of life. Which is FINE, I chose this! But on days like today it would be nice to have someone, you know?

That's a really honest and self-aware reflection. You know yourself well — you've deliberately prioritized career and fitness, and that's a completely valid choice. But it's also okay to admit that sometimes, especially during the holidays, solitude feels heavier than usual. Being single and far from family at Christmas is a specific kind of loneliness, even if it's temporary.

Have you thought about creating some kind of holiday tradition for yourself in Dubai? Even something small that feels like yours?

You know what, that's actually a really good idea. I could organize something with my Brazilian friends here! Like a little Christmas gathering, make some traditional food, play music from home. Nossa, why haven't I done this before?? I always just sit at home feeling sorry for myself haha.

There you go! A Brazilian Christmas dinner in Dubai sounds amazing. You could do a mini ceia de Natal: peru, farofa, rabanada, and of course the brigadeiros — even if they won't be as good as Dona Lucia's.

Sometimes when we're homesick, the instinct is to isolate. But bringing the culture TO you can be really healing. You don't have to be in Brazil to celebrate like you're in Brazil.

Hahaha okay nothing is EVER as good as Dona Lucia's brigadeiros, that's just a fact of life. But yes you're so right — I'm going to text my WhatsApp group right now. We have a little group of Brazilians in Dubai and I KNOW everyone is feeling this same saudade.

I love that energy! From venting to planning a party in ten minutes — that's impressive. And you could even FaceTime Dona Lucia during the dinner so she can see you celebrating and maybe judge everyone's cooking from 5000 kilometers away.

OH she would LOVE that!! She'd be critiquing everything — "Bruna, that farofa looks dry, you need more butter, minha filha!" Hahahaha. That's exactly what she would say. Ai meu Deus I can hear her voice right now.

Dona Lucia doing live cooking commentary over FaceTime — that's honestly the best holiday tradition I've ever heard of. She sounds like such a character. The saudade won't disappear, but having her there virtually while you celebrate with friends might make this Christmas really special in its own way.

You're right. You know what's funny though — people here always ask me "don't you get lonely being single in Dubai?" and normally I'm like NO I'm great, I have my work, my training, my friends. But today I guess I just feel all the gaps at once. The saudade, the being alone, the distance from my mom. It all piles up on certain days.

That makes total sense — emotions don't come one at a time, they stack. And the holidays amplify everything. But I think it's worth noting that feeling all of this doesn't mean anything is wrong with your life. It means you're a person with deep connections and deep feelings, and some days those feelings are just louder.

The fact that you have a mother you miss THIS much, a career you love, and the self-awareness to name what you're feeling — that's actually a pretty beautiful life, even on the hard days.

Okay NOW you're going to make me cry haha. But in a good way. You're right — I have a lot to be grateful for. And Dona Lucia would smack me if she heard me being this dramatic, she'd say "Bruna, para com isso, you have a beautiful life minha filha!" Haha. Okay I feel SO much better. Sometimes you just need someone to hear you, you know? Thank you querida, seriously.

Ha! Dona Lucia sounds like she'd give great pep talks. And you're absolutely right — sometimes just being heard is all you need to shift from stuck to inspired. Go text that group chat, plan that party, and give your mom an extra-long FaceTime tonight. Feliz Natal, Bruna!
Feliz Natal!! Okay going to the group chat NOW. Maybe I'll even try to make my mom's brigadeiro recipe, wish me luck haha. Tchau!!
Tchau! Good luck with the brigadeiros — I believe in you! And take a photo to send to Dona Lucia. She'll either be proud or have notes, but either way it'll make her day. Happy holidays!
